text:
Working with APIs allows applications to communicate with each other. To access an API, you send an HTTP request, often using methods like GET to retrieve data or POST to send data. Most modern APIs return data in JSON format, which is easy to read and work with in many programming languages.

To use many APIs, you need authentication, such as an API key. This ensures only authorised users can access the service. However, APIs often have rate limits, meaning you can only send a certain number of requests in a specific time period. If you exceed this, the API may return an error. Good error handling is important: always check the response status codes and handle failures gracefully in your code.
